name: Ghormeh Sabzi
description: A classic Persian dish made with fresh herbs, tender beef, kidney beans, and dried limes, known for its rich, tangy, and aromatic flavor.

instructions.1: In a large pot, sauté the chopped onion in a bit of oil until golden.

instructions.2: Add the cubed beef and turmeric, cooking until the meat is browned on all sides.

instructions.3: Add the chopped herbs and sauté for a few minutes until they wilt.

instructions.4: Pour in enough water to cover the ingredients, then add the dried limes.

instructions.5: Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for about 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the meat is tender.

instructions.6: Add the cooked kidney beans and continue to simmer for another 30 minutes.

instructions.7: Season with salt and pepper to taste before serving.

name: Gheymeh
description: A hearty Persian stew made with tender beef, yellow split peas, dried limes, and a tomato-based sauce, often garnished with crispy fried potatoes.

instructions.1: In a large pot, sauté the chopped onion in a bit of oil until golden.

instructions.2: Add the cubed beef and turmeric, cooking until the meat is browned on all sides.

instructions.3: Add the tomato paste and cook for a few minutes to develop the flavor.

instructions.4: Pour in enough water to cover the ingredients, then add the dried limes.

instructions.5: Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for about 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the meat is tender.

instructions.6: Add the cooked yellow split peas and continue to simmer for another 30 minutes.

instructions.7: Season with salt and pepper to taste before serving. Garnish with crispy fried potatoes on top.

name: Khoresh-e Esfenaj va Alu
description: A traditional Persian stew made with spinach, tender meat, and sweet-sour prunes, creating a rich and comforting flavor.

instructions.1: Heat oil in a pot and sauté the chopped onion until golden.

instructions.2: Add turmeric and meat; cook until the meat is browned.

instructions.3: Pour in water, cover, and simmer for about 60 minutes until the meat is tender.

instructions.4: In a separate pan, sauté the spinach until wilted and reduced in volume.

instructions.5: Add the spinach and prunes to the pot.

instructions.6: Season with salt and pepper to taste before serving.

instructions.7: Add lemon juice (optional) for a slightly tangy flavor.

name: Chelow (Persian Steamed Rice)
description: ََA fluffy Persian-style white rice with separated grains, often served with a crispy golden crust called tahdig.

instructions.1: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear to remove excess starch.

instructions.2: Soak the rice in salted water for at least 30 minutes, then drain.

instructions.3: Bring a large pot of water to a boil, add salt, and cook the rice for about 5-7 minutes until it's partially cooked (al dente).

instructions.4: Drain the rice and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process.

instructions.5: In a non-stick pot, heat oil or butter over medium heat. Add a layer of parboiled rice, then add a layer of potato slices or flatbread for tahdig.

instructions.6: Continue layering the remaining rice on top.

instructions.7: Cover the pot with a clean kitchen towel and place the lid on top to trap steam.

instructions.8: Cook on low heat for about 30-40 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and the tahdig is crispy and golden.

instructions.9: Serve the chelow with your choice of stew or grilled meat.

name: Kebab Tabei
description: A simple and juicy Persian-style minced meat kebab cooked in a pan instead of a grill, making it an easy homemade version of traditional kebab.

instructions.1: In a bowl, combine the minced meat with finely chopped onion, minced garlic, turmeric, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Mix well until all ingredients are evenly incorporated.

instructions.2: Divide the meat mixture into equal portions and shape each portion into a flat oval or round patty.

instructions.3: Heat a non-stick skillet or frying pan over medium heat. Place the patties in the pan and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until they are cooked through and have a nice browned crust.

instructions.4: Serve the kabab tabei with chelow (steamed rice) or flatbread, along with grilled tomatoes and fresh herbs.

name: Tahchin
description: َA famous Persian baked rice dish made with yogurt, eggs, saffron, and layered chicken or meat, known for its crispy golden crust and rich aroma.

instructions.1: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear to remove excess starch.

instructions.2: Soak the rice in salted water for at least 30 minutes, then drain.

instructions.3: In a bowl, mix the plain yogurt, eggs, dissolved saffron, turmeric, salt, and pepper. Add the shredded chicken to the mixture and stir to combine.

instructions.4: In a non-stick pot or baking dish, spread a thin layer of oil or butter on the bottom. Add a layer of parboiled rice, then spread a layer of the chicken and yogurt mixture on top.

instructions.5: Continue layering the remaining rice and chicken mixture until all ingredients are used.

instructions.6: Cover the pot with a clean kitchen towel and place the lid on top to trap steam.

instructions.7: Cook on low heat for about 30-40 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and the bottom layer forms a crispy golden crust.

instructions.8: Alternatively, you can bake it in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for about 45-60 minutes until fully cooked and golden brown.

instructions.9: Serve tahchin with your choice of stew or grilled meat.

name: Kotlet
description: A crispy, golden fried patties made from minced meat, potatoes, and spices, commonly served as a quick and delicious Persian street-style meal.

instructions.1: Boil the potatoes until tender, then mash them and let them cool.

instructions.2: In a large bowl, combine the minced meat, mashed potatoes, finely chopped onions, minced garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper. Mix well until all ingredients are evenly incorporated.

instructions.3: Shape the mixture into oval or round patties.

instructions.4: Heat oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Fry the patties in batches until they are golden brown and cooked through on both sides.

instructions.5: Drain the fried kotlet on paper towels to remove excess oil.

instructions.6: Serve hot with bread, fresh herbs, or a side of yogurt.

name: Kookoo Sabzi
description: A traditional Persian herb-based omelet made with fresh herbs and eggs, often served cold or warm as a light meal or appetizer.

instructions.1: In a large bowl, beat the eggs and season with salt, pepper, and turmeric.

instructions.2: Add the chopped herbs, onions, and walnuts (if using) to the beaten eggs. Mix well to combine.

instructions.3: Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Pour the egg and herb mixture into the skillet, spreading it out evenly.

instructions.4: Cook on medium-low heat until the bottom is set and golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.5: Carefully flip the kookoo using a large plate or lid to help turn it over. Cook the other side until golden brown and fully set, about another 5-7 minutes.

instructions.6: Serve warm or at room temperature with bread or yogurt on the side.

name: Kookoo Sibzamini
description: A popular Persian dish made with mashed potatoes, eggs, and spices, pan-fried until crispy on the outside and soft inside.

instructions.1: In a large bowl, beat the eggs and season with salt, pepper, and paprika.

instructions.2: Add the mashed potatoes, onions, and garlic to the beaten eggs. Mix well to combine.

instructions.3: Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Pour the egg and potato mixture into the skillet, spreading it out evenly.

instructions.4: Cook on medium-low heat until the bottom is set and golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.5: Carefully flip the kookoo using a large plate or lid to help turn it over. Cook the other side until golden brown and fully set, about another 5-7 minutes.

instructions.6: Serve warm or at room temperature with bread or yogurt on the side.

name: Joojeh Kabab
description: A popular Persian dish made with marinated chicken, grilled to juicy perfection with a tangy saffron flavor.

instructions.1: In a bowl, combine the plain yogurt, lemon juice, dissolved saffron, minced garlic, grated onion, salt, and pepper. Mix well to create the marinade.

instructions.2: Add the chicken pieces to the marinade and coat them thoroughly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight for best flavor.

instructions.3: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat. Thread the marinated chicken pieces onto skewers.

instructions.4: Grill the chicken skewers for about 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and has a nice char.

instructions.5: Serve the joojeh kabab with chelow (steamed rice) or flatbread, along with grilled tomatoes and fresh herbs.

name: Chelow Kebab
description: One of the most famous Persian dishes, consisting of fragrant saffron rice served with juicy grilled meat kebabs, often accompanied by grilled tomatoes.

ingredients.name: Oil
ingredients.quantity: as required for frying or grilling

instructions.1: Prepare the kebab by mixing the minced meat or chicken with chopped onion, minced garlic, dissolved saffron, salt, and pepper. Shape the mixture into kebabs.

instructions.2: Grill or pan-fry the kebabs until they are cooked through and have a nice char.

instructions.3: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear to remove excess starch.

instructions.4: Soak the rice in salted water for at least 30 minutes, then drain.

instructions.5: Cook the rice in boiling salted water until it's partially cooked (al dente), then drain and rinse with cold water.

instructions.6: In a non-stick pot, heat oil and add a layer of parboiled rice. Continue layering the remaining rice on top.

instructions.7: Cover the pot with a clean kitchen towel and place the lid on top to trap steam. Cook on low heat for about 30-40 minutes until the rice is fully cooked and has a crispy golden crust (tahdig).

instructions.8: Serve the chelow with the grilled kebabs, along with grilled tomatoes and fresh herbs.

name: Ash Reshteh
description: A hearty Persian soup made with herbs, beans, noodles, and topped with fried onions, garlic, and mint

instructions.1: In a bowl, combine the plain yogurt, lemon juice, dissolved saffron, minced garlic, grated onion, salt, and pepper. Mix well to create the marinade.

instructions.2: Add the lamb chops to the marinade and coat them thoroughly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight for best flavor.

instructions.3: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat. Grill the lamb chops for about 4-5 minutes on each side for medium-rare, or longer if you prefer them more well-done.

instructions.4: Serve the shishlik with chelow (steamed rice) or flatbread, along with grilled vegetables and fresh herbs.
